observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, instruct the Municipal Health Office to (a) coordinate with the Provincial Government and DOH RO III for the noted difference on the transferred vaccines; (b) conduct thorough review of the masterlist of vaccinated individuals and make the necessary corrections; and (c) henceforth, ensure that the information recorded on records and reports are complete and accurate.

We recommended that the Acting Municipal Treasurer (a) at the end of every month, reconcile the cashbook balances with the bank balances and accounting records; (b) ensure the correctness and accuracy of entries recorded in the cashbooks; (c) prepare and submit the Report of Checks Issued (RCI) pursuant to the requirements of the NGAS Manual for LGUs; and (d) make the necessary adjustments in the cashbooks.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant and Acting Municipal Treasurer closely coordinate and regularly reconcile their respective records for the early detection and correction of errors.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive agreed to (a) instruct the concerned accountable personnel to liquidate their cash advances as soon as the purposes were served; and (b) require the Municipal Accountant for the immediate submission of the liquidation documents with the transaction mentioned under Item 3.4.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) create an Inventory Committee to facilitate the conduct of the physical count of all inventory items semestrally to establish the correct balances for each account and submit a complete and accurate Report on the Physical Count of Inventory; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant and Acting General Services Officer to prepare and maintain complete stock and supplies ledger cards for all inventories.
Further, we recommended that Local Chief Executive direct all department heads and designated supply officers to (a) adopt a systematic policy congruent with regulations in the flow of documents that will capture the faithful representation of accounting events and transactions on inventories and related accounts; (b) facilitate a training to orient employees in charge of inventories on the rules and regulations in the management of inventory specifically on the maintenance of the required inventory records and on the prompt submission of inventory reports; and (c) ensure the preparation and timely submission of reports pertaining to the issuance of inventory items.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) instruct the personnel in-charge of preparation of journal entry vouchers and in the recording of transactions to properly analyze each transaction before recording in the books to ensure proper recognition and de-recognition of inventories in the books of account; and (b) make the necessary adjusting entries called for based on the foregoing deficiencies noted.

We reiterated our recommendations that the:
Management create an inventory committee who will conduct a complete physical inventory count of all the PPE owned by the Municipality pursuant to COA Circular 2020-006;
Acting Municipal Treasurer and Municipal Accountant (a) maintain complete property and ledger cards; and (b) reconcile the reported PPE items in the inventory report and accounting records; and
Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Acting Municipal Treasurer, identify the PPEs with no available records.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant instruct the personnel in charge of the recording of transactions to coordinate with the Municipal Engineering Office for the proper recording of expenditures incurred for infrastructure projects.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant make the necessary adjusting entries to reflect the correct balance of the PPE account.

We recommended that the Municipal Development Council, chaired by the Local Chief Executive, (a) strengthen the cycle of planning, execution, monitoring and evaluation of programs and projects for prioritization under the 20% DF in order to ensure well prepared and implementation-ready programs and projects; and (b) ensure that projects programmed for implementation are capital investments rather than on “as needed basis”.
Further, we recommended that Municipal Development Council and Municipal Budget Officer ensure that the specific projects proposed for local funding are included in the Annual Budget and Annual Investment Program in accordance with applicable laws.

We recommended that the Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Council prepare a comprehensive and an in-depth LDRRMFIP, both for current and previous years’ LDRRMF.
Moreover, we recommended that the LDRRM Officer and Municipal Accountant (a) ensure that all DRRMF are included in the Report on Utilization of DRRMF for easy monitoring of the utilization of the fund; and (b) prepare all required reports pursuant to COA Circular Nos. 2012-002 and 2014-002.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the implementing departments to monitor the implementation of programs and projects to ensure timely completion and avoid non-compliance with the conditions of the transfers and lessen the eventual return of funds.
Further, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer to return the unutilized funds from the NDRRMF to the National Treasury.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant maintain subsidiary ledgers for each account of the General Ledger pursuant to COA Circular 94-013 and the NGAS Manual.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, in coordination with the Municipal GAD Focal Point System, ensure that PPAs implemented from the GAD fund are responsive to the gender issues and/or gaps actually faced by its constituents and employees.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive (LCE) agreed, as Chairman of the LSB, the Co-Chairman and other members of the LSB (a) conduct consultation/discussion with heads of schools and other stakeholders for strategic prioritization in the allocation of the SEF and to properly plan the PPAs to be implemented from the fund; and (b) coordinate with the Acting Municipal Treasurer, Municipal Budget Officer and Municipal Accountant for the available SEF and maximize the utilization of the Fund to attain its goals and objectives.

We recommended that the Bids and Awards Committee strictly observe the provisions of RA No. 9184 and COA Circular 2012-001 especially on (a) the completeness of the documents supporting the procurement activities, contract and disbursement vouchers; and (b) evaluation of bidding documents.
Further, we recommended that the Municipal Engineering Office (a) ensure that the required detailed engineering activities are carried out properly to guarantee the correct computation of the project requirements; and (b) as implementing department for infrastructure projects, ensure compliance with the reporting and posting requirements of COA Circular 2013-004.
Moreover, we recommended that the Municipal Accountant, in coordination with the Municipal Engineer, ensure that all payments to contractors are properly supported with the required documents.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive instructed all requesting departments and the Bids and Awards Committee to exert efforts in the determination and evaluation of the estimated unit cost of the items being requested giving due consideration to the current and prevailing market prices pursuant to Sections 20 and 36 of the IRR of RA No. 9184 to ensure that the most advantageous price is obtained.

We recommended and the Local Chief Executive instructed the Municipal Engineer (a) to closely monitor the implementation of all projects; (b) to create monitoring team that will regularly check the condition of completed projects for early detection of defects; (c) together with the Contractor concerned, to conduct inspection and thorough assessment of the projects found with defects to determine the appropriate repair/maintenance treatment pursuant to the guidelines for the maintenance of and rehabilitation of unreinforced concrete roads prescribed under DPWH Department Order No. 45 series of 2006; and (d) to require the Contractor concerned to cause the repair of the portion of the projects found to be defective at no additional cost to the Municipality.

We recommended that Local Chief Executive, instruct the Bids and Awards Committee and other concerned offices to (a) submit the unsubmitted contracts and POs for evaluation and review; and (b) furnish the Audit Team with copy of the purchase orders and contract documents not later than five working days from the issuance of the purchase orders and perfections of contracts together with duly accomplished supporting documents.

We reiterated our recommendation that Management, through the Municipal Social Welfare and Development Office and Sangguniang Bayan, establish written policy guidelines and eligibility criteria in the granting of financial assistance to ensure that the program’s implementation is in accordance with the prescribed rules and regulations.
We also recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the concerned municipal offices to (a) strictly comply with the rules and guidelines in the grant of financial assistance particularly on the required supporting documents; (b) submit an explanation on the inconsistencies noted between the recorded assistance and those allegedly received by the beneficiaries; (c) ensure that all checks shall only be released to the named payee or duly authorized representative; and (d) devise a coordinated system in the assessment, evaluation and actual release of the financial assistance to ensure that all the beneficiaries are eligible, transactions are properly documented and assistance are received by the beneficiaries.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to (a) instruct the personnel in charge of payroll preparation and remittances to conduct monthly reconciliation of employer and employees’ share on premiums/contributions and deductions for loan amortizations and taxes and the corresponding remittances; (b) conduct thorough analysis of the Due to BIR, GSIS, HDMF, and PhilHealth to evaluate the prior year’s unremitted balances as well as the negative balance of Due to GSIS account to determine if these are valid obligations of the Municipality; and (c) comply with the applicable tax laws and regulations particularly on the tax rate that should be used for withholding taxes.
In addition, we also recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) instruct the assigned AAO to immediately report/communicate to the GSIS any adjustments on the salary of the employees; and (b) instruct the personnel in charge of remittance to strictly comply with the pertinent laws, rules, and regulations on the timely and full remittance of premiums/contributions and loan amortization to avoid payment of penalties and interest.

We recommended and the Acting Municipal Treasurer agreed to (a) strictly adhere with the provisions of the NGAS Manual pertaining to the policies and procedures on disbursements; and (b) stop the practice of preparing checks without the certification of the Municipal Accountant on the disbursement voucher to ensure the validity and completeness of supporting documents.
Further, we recommended and the Municipal Accountant agreed to instruct (a) the accounting personnel in charge of uploading checks in the bank’s online facility to upload only checks with signed Accountant’s Advice; and (b) instruct all accounting personnel to forward all disbursement vouchers to her for review and certification before preparation of check.

We recommended and the Municipal Accountant and Municipal Budget Officer agreed to comply with the preparation and submission of SCBAA pursuant to IPSAS 24 – Presentation of Budget Information in the Financial Statements.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive: 
create an inventory committee to (i) conduct physical count; (ii) require all offices to submit a list of all properties assigned to them to facilitate the physical inventory; and (iii) coordinate with the Accounting Office to determine the PPEs with available detailed records;
require the Municipal Treasurer to coordinate with the Municipal Accountant to maintain complete and updated file of property cards and acknowledgement receipts of equipment; and
require the Municipal Accountant to determine the PPE for reclassification to inventory accounts for the correct valuation of the PPE accounts.

MDRRMO  (i) prepare a comprehensive and an in-depth LDRRMP encompassing the four (4) thematic areas of disaster risk reduction and management such as disaster prevention and mitigation, disaster preparedness, disaster response, and disaster rehabilitation and recovery in alignment with the recommended activities contained in the Comprehensive Municipal Disaster Risk Reduction Management (MDRRM) and Climate Change Adaptation (CCA) Plan; (ii) submit the LDRRMP to the Local Sanggunian for approval through the Local DRRM Council for their review; and (iii) organize and conduct training, orientation and knowledge management activities on disaster risk reduction and management at the local level on a continuing basis to maintain, provide or arrange the provision of, or to otherwise make available, suitably-trained and competent personnel for effective civil defense and disaster risk reduction and management in its area; c. Municipal Budget Officer see to it that all charges in the LDRRMF are calamity/disaster related expenses and in consonance with the approved programs/activities in the LDRRMP; and d. Municipal Accountant together with the Municipal Treasurer (i) facilitate the transfer of unexpended balance of the LDRMF to the Trust Fund accounts of ₱1,396,625.05; and (ii) install an effective internal control in the recording, maintenance and distribution of relief items and other disaster supplies in accordance with the above-mentioned rules and regulations.
observations.managementAction: Local Climate Change Action Plan (LCCAP) for the period 2017-2019 was formulated. The MDRRMO prepared an annual plan adopted by SB under Resolution No. 13-2017 dated February 27, 2017. Responders attended trainings on Standard First Aid, CPR and AED, and Water Search and Rescue although, still inadequate. Transfer of funds was made in February 2017. An inventory report of stocks at the end of the year was prepared by the MDDRMO and distributions sheets were filed by the MSWDO.
observations.reason: The Municipality complied with the recommendation on updating its LCCAP with an omission to identify the recommended program/projects/activities.; Responders attended trainings on Standard First Aid, CPR and AED, and Water Search and Rescue although, still inadequate.; The Accounting Office did not maintain supplies ledger cards.; Transfer of funds was made in February 2017. An inventory report of stocks at the end of the year was prepared by the MDDRMO and distributions sheets were filed by the MSWDO.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Members of the Municipal Local School Board allocate the SEF budget only for eligible and priority program, projects and activities allowed by the law and encourage schools to make use of the DepEd’s funds for their regular operations; Members of the Municipal Local School Board and Municipal Budget Officer ensure that all charges in the SEF are within the adherence to the provisions of R.A. 5447, Section 100 and 272, RA 7160 and DepEd-DBM-DILG Joint Circular No. 01, s. 2017 dated January 19, 2017 in the preparation of the CY 2018 SEF budget and in the utilization of the CY 2017 Local School Board Fund; Members of the Municipal Local School Board thru the Municipal Accountant and Treasurer, allow only the grant of cash advances for time-bound activities to duly designated disbursing officers who are bonded; and Members of the Municipal Local School Board submit above-cited lacking documents and henceforth, ensure that all claims are supported by proper reports and henceforth, ensure that all claims are supported by proper reports and comply with the required documentation for similar transactions.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the (a) Local Chief Executive require the Local Development Council to propose in the 20% Development Fund projects which are in the nature of investment or capital expenditures pursuant to the guidelines set out under Memorandum Circular No. 2011 to attain the developmental intent of the Fund; and (b) Municipal Engineer and other heads of implementing offices ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.
observations.managementAction: The amount of the allocation of P2,800,000.00 for PPAs not included among the priority listed in the Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM, as amended by JMC No. 2017-1 had decreased as compared with the amount ₱3,081,795.60 in 2016. observations.reason: The amount of the allocation of P2,800,000.00 for PPAs not included among the priority listed in the Joint Memorandum Circular (JMC) No. 2011-1 dated April 13, 2011 of the DILG and DBM, as amended by JMC No. 2017-1 had decreased as compared with the amount ₱3,081,795.60 in 2016.; Based on the registry, the allocations for infrastructure projects under the 20% Development Fund were utilized as of year-end.

observations.recommendation: We commended the Municipality for its being substantially compliant and recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with the Sangguniang Bayan continue implementing the rules, ways and strategies in order to help preserve the environment and achieve the objectives of the RA No. 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality continued to be substantially compliant with the provisions of the Republic Act No. 9003 under its solid waste management program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to remit the taxes withheld within the prescribed period as provided in Revenue Memorandum Circular 23-2012.
observations.managementAction: Records showed that the amounts due to the BIR for CY 2017 were under remitted for the period of January, February, May, June, July and August. The Municipal Treasurer already remitted the withheld taxes to the BIR within the prescribed period from September to December, 2017.
observations.reason: Records showed that the amounts due to the BIR for CY 2017 were under remitted for the period of January, February, May, June, July and August.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer to deposit all collections regularly and intact to the authorized government depository banks as required under existing regulations; and
observations.managementAction: The Municipal Treasurer is directed to deposit regularly and intact to comply with the requirements of the law.
observations.reason: The delayed preparation of summary report of collections and deposits due to multiple workloads by the Liquidating Officer for collections caused the Municipal Treasurer to not regularly deposit all collections.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that: The General Services Officer and all other concerned Municipal Officials take appropriate action to secure proof of ownerships on all lands or real properties owned by the Municipality. Also, the Office of the Accountant together with the Inventory Committee thru the Treasurer's/General Services Office reconcile the respective records to come up with complete, accurate and reliable Land and Land Improvements and Building and Structures accounts; and The Municipal Accountant and Municipal Treasurer's Office/GSO be required to maintain property ledger cards and property cards to support these accounts.
observations.managementAction: Management is in the process of applying the titles of the remaining untitled properties.
observations.reason: Application of the titles is still on-going for free patents. Also, lots occupied by schools are untitled because they are still in the hands of the previous owners. Also, Property Officer could not function fully because of her concurrent position. (see finding no. 1)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Members of the Municipal Local School Board: Prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and Request for inputs from the Deped official/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the Deped criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund.
observations.managementAction: The Local School Board committed to adhere with the recommendations on the budgeting, allocation and utilization of the funds and conform to the provisions thereof.
observations.reason: Non-compliance with the funds priority spending, since the Local School Board still charge electricity, various travelling and training expenses for teacher development to SEF fund instead of regular school fund as the District has no more fund support from the Division to achieve quality outcomes for the schools. (see finding no. 3)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Local Chief Executive require the: Personnel Officer to hire contract of service/job order personnel based on necessity who should be identified/attributed to specific local projects; and Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements of funds for services rendered by casual, contract of service and job order personnel are supported with the necessary and appropriate supporting documents to establish validity and propriety of payment.
observations.managementAction: Additional work force was necessary on office-based works for optimum public service.
observations.reason: Additional work force was necessary on office-based works for optimum public service.

observations.recommendation: We commended Management of its efforts to uplift the living condition of the poor.  We still recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: Municipal Social Welfare Officer to ensure that the nutritional status of the minimal number of under-nourished children improve to normal and exert more efforts to maintain or improve further the large number of children within the normal weight; and As far as practicable, considering the maintaining balance of the account, Municipal Treasurer to comply with Section 2.4 of DSWD Administrative Order No. 08 Series of 2012 on the opening of a separate bank account for the Program.
observations.reason: Opening of separate bank account would cause difficulties considering that the Municipality already maintaining numerous accounts.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct the Supply Officer/ Municipal Treasurer to procure commonly-used supplies directly from the PS-DBM.
observations.reason: Management did not procure supplies from PS-DBM due to the non-availability of supplies and stocks and poor quality.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) enjoin all agency officials and employees to file their claims with the Accounting Office only when supported with the necessary documents to support their validity and regularity; and (b) direct the Municipal Accountant to perform diligent review of the completeness of documents and proper accomplishment of forms/reports supporting the claims before endorsing disbursement vouchers to the approving official for payment.
observations.reason: See findings 3 and 9

observations.recommendation: 4.10	We recommended that the (a) BAC Secretariat include the procurement of common-use supplies from the PS-DBM in the Municipality’s Annual Procurement Plan; and (b) General Services Officer procure its common-use supplies at the PS-DBM and employ the alternative procurement method of shopping only when conditions are satisfied in accordance with the provisions of Section 52.1 of the RIRR.

5.9	We recommended that the (a) Local Chief Executive require the Local Development Council to propose in the 20% Development Fund projects which are in the nature of investment or capital expenditures pursuant to the guidelines set out under Memorandum Circular No. 2011 to attain the  developmental intent of the Fund; and (b) Municipal Engineer and other heads of implementing offices ensure the effective and efficient implementation of the proposed projects within their targeted completion period through close monitoring and supervision to maximize the developmental benefits and the early enjoyment thereof by the intended beneficiaries.

6.13	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the GAD Focal Person to comply with the provisions of PCW, DILG, DBM and NEDA Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2013-01 and COA Circular No. 2014-001 particularly on the conduct of gender audit and gender mainstreaming in the Municipality.

7.7	We commended the Municipality for its being substantially compliant and recommended that the Local Chief Executive together with the Sangguniang Bayan to continue implementing the rules, ways and strategies in order to help preserve the environment and achieve the objectives of the RA No. 9003.

8.8	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the OIC-Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to comply strictly with government regulations that all payments must be made through checks payable directly to suppliers/sellers except for petty expenditures, so that the corresponding taxes may be deducted, withheld and remitted due to the government and for internal control purposes to avoid the opportunity for possible abuse or misuse of government funds.

9.21	We recommended that
a.  the Members of the Bids and Awards Committee and Technical Working Group (i) henceforth, strictly observe the procedures in the procurement through public bidding particularly in the verification and evaluation of the submitted documents of participating bidders; (ii) render an explanation on the absence of the CSO representatives in the bidding process; and (iii) henceforth, comply strictly with the sending of the invitation to the required number of CSO representatives as CSO observers during the procurement process particularly during the opening of bids; and
b. the Municipal Accountant (i) comply, along with other offices/officials concerned, with the submission of the lacking documents/information as discussed above; and (ii) henceforth, perform a diligent review of the completeness of documents and such other data required pertinent to the transactions to substantiate their payments.

10.4	We recommended that the Local Chief Executive instruct the Municipal Treasurer and the Municipal Accountant to remit the taxes withheld within the prescribed period as provided in Revenue Memorandum Circular 23-2012.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive, thru the Accountant and Treasurer, allow only the grant of cash advances for time-bound activities to duly designated disbursing officers who are bonded pursuant to the provisions of COA Circular No. 97-002 regarding cash advances and Section 101 of Presidential Decree No. 1445 on the bonding of accountable officers.
observations.reason: The Municipality still granted cash advances to official s not designated as disbursing officers in some isolated cases (see finding no.3)

observations.recommendation: The Management establish petty cash fund to answer for the non-recurring, emergency and petty expenses of the Municipality in compliance with Section 48 of the Manual on New Government Accounting System.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant (a) exhaust possible efforts to locate the missing documents affecting the subject accounts; (b) initiate efforts to request authority from the COA for the write-off and/or adjustment of the account balances supported by the required documents as provided in COA Circular 97-001; and (c) provide impairment allowance for the receivable accounts starting for CY 2016, upon receipt of the pertinent guidelines thereto under the Local Government Accounting Manual.
observations.managementAction: The accounts were already adjusted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the: Treasury personnel in charge of the accounts to monitor the amount to be automatically retained with the LGU to be  used for the OBO’s programs and projects and the amount to be remitted to BTr representing the DPWH’s 5% share; Municipal Treasurer and the OBO to closely coordinate for the proper implementation of the OBO’s programs and projects using the Trust Fund in accordance with the pertinent law and regulations; and Municipal Engineer, acting as the OBO, to exercise prudence in the planning and implementation of programs and projects using the TF and properly observe the guidelines on the preparation of the program of works.

observations.recommendation: We commended management of its efforts to achieve effective planning, implementation and monitoring of the Agency’s GAD PPAs. However, we recommended that proper coordination with the different barangays and other concerned officials be closely made to effectively attain projects and programs proposed in the GAD Plan to improve further the accomplishment rate of the Municipality to address prevalent gender issues.

observations.managementAction: The Municipality has complied with DILG-DBM Joint Memorandum Circular No. 2011-1 dated April 11, 2011 on the appropriation of 20% Development Fund of P20,038,144.00, registering a utilization rate of 76.83% or P15,396,665.43 in CY 2015.
observations.reason: The PPAs were not all implemented due to administration transition period and election ban, but the allotted funds were already realigned to other Municipal projects. (see finding no. 5)

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Municipal Accountant comply strictly with Section 5.1.5 of COA Circular No. 2012-002 dated September 12, 2012 to monitor and evaluate the utilization of LDRRMF based on the programs, projects and activities listed in the LDRRMP as incorporated in the local development plans and annual work and financial plan to ensure the optimum implementation of the pre-disaster preparedness programs.
observations.managementAction: Monthly Report on the Sources and Utilization of the Local Disaster Risk Management Fund for CY 2016 were prepared and submitted.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the: Local Chief Executive require the: Personnel Officer to hire contract of service/job order personnel based on necessity who should be identified/attributed to specific local projects; and Municipal Accountant to ensure that disbursements of funds for services rendered by casual, contract of service and job order personnel are supported with the necessary and appropriate supporting documents to establish validity and propriety of payment.
observations.managementAction: The concerned personnel were required to submit accomplishment reports.

observations.recommendation: To further improve its performance in complying with RA 9003, we recommended that the Local Chief Executive (a) encourage all remaining component barangays to pass the necessary ordinances for the protection of the environment; and (b) request the Municipality’s MENRO to extend technical assistance to the barangays concerned, if necessary, to pave the way for their compliance to the basic requirements of RA 9003.
observations.managementAction: The Municipality has substantially complied with RA No. 9003 by taking steps to establish its ecological solid waste management program.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive require the Municipal Treasurer and/or Supply Officer to furnish COA with copies of all purchase orders/contracts and all papers relative thereto within five (5) days after the perfection thereof as well as delivery receipts and inspection reports to afford the Audit Team to perform timely verification of deliveries.
observations.managementAction: Management complied with the submission of POs/Contracts and other documents relative thereto but failed to submit it within the prescribed period.
observations.reason: Management complied with the submission of POs/Contractsand other documents relative thereto but failed to submit it within the prescribed period.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the members of the BAC Secretariat fully utilize the PhilGEPS (Philippine Government Electronic Procurement System) and post all procurement opportunities to attract more qualified bidders for a more competitive bidding in adherence to the principle of transparency, efficiency and economy in the procurement process.
observations.managementAction: During the year, the Municipality partly complied with the utilization of PhilGEPS.
observations.reason: PhilGEPS was not utilized for all the Municipality’s procurement activities particularly on goods which were purchased on per request basis through shopping.

observations.recommendation: We recommended that the Local Chief Executive direct all the Disbursing Officers to stamp “PAID” or perforate all disbursement vouchers and their supporting documents to preclude the possible re-use of the same.
